title: Correlation vs Causation
script_summary: Exploring the difference between correlation and causation reveals critical insights into how we interpret data.

segments.voiceover: Here's what science actually says about correlation vs causation.
Just because two things happen together doesn't mean one causes the other.
Take ice cream sales and shark attacks. They both spike in summer. But do they cause each other?
Researchers note that correlation merely highlights relationships, not causation. Random factors could be at play.
Understanding this helps prevent misleading conclusions about data, leading to better decisions.
Want to dive deeper into data science? Hit that follow button!
